name: Honda Civic Type R
reliability: 8/10 — robust K20C turbo and Honda bones; track abuse exposes gearbox and cooling limits
cost_to_own: Insurance: High (performance hatch, theft target, dealer markups) · Maintenance ~$450/yr
popular_mods: Intake, downpipe, catback exhaust, upgraded front-mount intercooler
Big brake pads + high-temp fluid for track days
Reinforced 3rd/4th gears for heavily tracked cars

generations.EK9 / EP3 / FD2 (1997–2010): JDM/Euro icons (B16B, K20A) — naturally aspirated, high-revving legends. Never sold new in the US.
generations.FK2 (2015–2017): First turbocharged Type R, 306hp K20C1. Europe and Japan only.
generations.FK8 (2017–2021): First US-market CTR. 306hp, set the FWD Nürburgring lap record. Bold boy-racer styling. Watch fuel pump and 3rd/4th gear on tracked cars.
generations.FL5 (2023–present): 315hp, 15% stiffer chassis, more mature looks. Reclaimed the FWD 'Ring record. Widely called the best-driving CTR yet.

common_issues: FK8 in-tank fuel pump failure (recalled) — rev-match/emissions codes
3rd/4th gear failure on heavily tracked FK8s (reinforced gears available)
Clutch wear if driven hard; rev-hang on upshifts (both gens)
maintenance_tips: Confirm the FK8 fuel pump recall was completed on used examples
Track use: add oil/diff cooling and monitor temps to avoid limp mode
Upgrade brake fluid and pads before track days
Check oil for dilution in cold climates (shared K-series turbo trait)
